questions.question: Whom did the king decide to approach to find answers to his three questions?

questions.question: What was the hermit's reaction to hearing the king's three questions?

questions.question: Who came running towards the king when he was digging the ground in front of the hermit's hut in the plot, Three questions?
questions.explanation: The king was digging the ground when he saw a bearded man come running towards the hermit's house. The man was badly injured and blood was oozing out of his stomach.

questions.question: What did the king and hermit do on seeing the injured man in the plot, Three questions?
questions.explanation: The king and the hermit immediately took action on seeing blood flowing from the injured man's stomach. They hurriedly unfastened his clothes and attended his wound.

questions.question: What did the king give the injured man to drink? (Three questions)
questions.explanation: The injured man felt better after his wound was re-dressed and he asked for something to drink, so, the king gave him a glass of freshwater to drink.

questions.question: Why did the bearded man follow the king to the hermit's house?

options.option: He too wanted to meet the hermit
He wanted to protect the king from any danger
He wanted to attack the king
He was a spy from neighbouring kingdom

questions.explanation: The king had killed the bearded man's brother and taken away his property, so, to avenge his brother's death the bearded man followed the king. The man knew that it was a good opportunity to kill the king as he knew that the king had gone alone to see the hermit.

The hermit received only common folks
The king wanted to see the hermit secretly

questions.explanation: The king got off his horse and went on foot to meet the hermit for he was well aware of the fact that the hermit received only common folks, so, to appear like a commoner he left his horse with his bodyguard and went on alone.

questions.question: Who wounded the bearded man in the plot, Three questions?
questions.explanation: The bearded man was following the king to kill him to avenge his brother's death but the king's bodyguard spotted him and wounded him on recognition.

questions.explanation: The king sent his messengers throughout his kingdom, promising a large sum of money to anyone who would answer his three questions.

questions.question: How were the pair of slippers projected to be in the plot, A pair of slippers?

questions.question: Where was the newly found kitten kept by Mridu's cousins?

questions.question: Who was Mahendran in the plot, A gift of chappals?

questions.explanation: Thatha was Mridu's grandfather with whom she lived in Chennai in the story.

questions.question: To whom were the music master's slippers gifted by Mridu and her cousins?
questions.explanation: The innocent children pitied the beggar and gifted him the music master's odd-looking chappals. The beggar was barefoot and had blisters all under his feet, so, Mridu and her cousins gifted him the music master's chappals as they were a perfect fit for him.

questions.question: How did Rukku Manni feel when Lalli told her about the missing chappals ​​​​​​?

questions.question: Why was everyone in the kingdom talking about the Hilsa fish?

options.option: It was the season for Hilsa fish
The fish triggered some allergies in the human body
There was extreme scarcity of the fish
The price of Hilsa fish had increased drastically

questions.explanation: It was the season for Hilsa fish, so, everyone around was talking about it. The fish was easily available in the season and the fishmongers were selling only Hilsa fish and trying their best to lure the customers around to buy it.

questions.question: How was Gopal projected to be in the plot, Gopal and the Hilsa fish?
questions.explanation: Gopal was the wisest man in the king's court and was well known for his wisdom and presence of mind.

questions.question: How did the king feel when everyone in his kingdom talked about the Hilsa fish?
questions.explanation: The king felt irritated by hearing everyone in his kingdom talking about the Hilsa fish. He lost his temper and believed that nobody could stop the people of his kingdom from talking and discussing the fish.

questions.question: What did Gopal do before he went to buy the Hilsa fish?
questions.explanation: Gopal dressed up in a very shabby and peculiar manner to capture everybody's attention, before going out to buy his Hilsa fish.

questions.question: Why did Gopal dress up shabbily before he went out to buy the Hilsa fish?

options.option: He was going for a fancy dress competition
The king had ordered him to dress up in an odd manner

questions.explanation: Gopal shaved his half-face, wore rags, and smeared ash over his body. He dressed up in this peculiar fashion to grab everyone's attention, he wanted to divert people from talking about the Hilsa fish.

questions.question: Who prompted and tried to stop Gopal from going to the market, dressed up in a disgraceful manner?
questions.explanation: Gopal's wife was shocked to see her husband dressed up so weirdly for going to the market to buy the Hilsa fish. She tried her level best to stop him from stepping outside the house in that peculiar manner but failed to do so.

questions.question: Why did Gopal want to buy the Hilsa fish?

options.option: Its price had gone down drastically
His wife wanted to gift it to her relative
He himself loved to eat the fish

questions.explanation: Gopal had accepted the king's challenge of bringing the Hilsa fish to his court without anyone asking him about the fish or avoiding the buzz that existed about the fish in the kingdom that season.

questions.question: How did the fishmongers try to lure the customers to buy the Hilsa fish?

options.option: By advertising the benefits of eating it
By saying that the price of fish has gone down

questions.explanation: The fishmongers sold the Hilsa fish in bulk as it was the season for it. They tried to lure the customers by stating that the price of Hilsa fish had gone down for that particular day.

questions.question: Which fish did Gopal intend to buy from the market?
questions.explanation: It was the season for Hilsa fish and Gopal had accepted the king's challenge of bringing a Hilsa fish to the court without anyone asking him a word about the fish, so, Gopal went to the market to buy the Hilsa fish for the king.

questions.question: What did Gopal put on his face while dressing up to buy a Hilsa fish?
questions.explanation: Gopal smeared his face and body with ash before he went out to the market to buy the Hilsa fish. He did so to capture everyone's attention with his odd dressing up manner.

questions.question: What did people think about Gopal when he dressed up weirdly while shopping for the Hilsa fish?

He was believed to be a mystic

questions.explanation: People in the market found Gopal's dressing sense to be quite comical. Some passerby thought that he was a mad man and others believed him to be a mystic.

questions.explanation: Gopal was a wise man and he knew how to capture everyone's attention. He began to sing and dance so loudly that it grabbed the king's attention and he wanted to see the man who was behaving crazily outside his palace.

questions.question: How did the king react when he realized that Gopal had won the challenge?

chapters.name: The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom

questions.question: How was the old couple projected to be in the plot, The ashes that made trees bloom?
questions.explanation: The old couple in the story was very honest and hardworking. The kind couple loved their pet dog a lot and was generous towards all.

questions.question: Which animal was kept as a pet by the old couple in the plot, The ashes that made the trees bloom?
questions.explanation: The old couple had a little dog as their pet. The couple was very fond of it and took good care of the dog.

questions.question: What was the name of the old couple's pet dog? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.explanation: Muko was the name given by the old couple to their pet dog. The couple had no children, so, they loved Muko as though it was a baby.

questions.question: Who followed the old man to work every day in the plot, The ashes made trees bloom?
questions.explanation: Every day the dog followed his master, a rice farmer to his work, into the fields where the old man worked hard from morning till the sunset.

questions.question: What did the old man find as he struck his hoe in the earth in the plot, The ashes that made trees bloom?
questions.explanation: The dog convinced and made his master dig a particular spot in the ground and as the old man struck his hoe in the earth, a pile of gold gleamed before him.

questions.question: What did the dog do on reaching the spot where the treasure was hidden? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.explanation: The dog began a lively scratching on the spot where it had managed to make it's master follow too because of its actions. The dog hinted the old man of something buried inside the ground on that particular spot by scratching it.

questions.question: What did the old couple do after they found the pile of gold? (The ashes that made trees bloom)

options.option: They bought a piece of land
They gave plentifully to their poor neighbours
They made a feast for their friends

questions.explanation: The old couple was very kind and generous, so, with their new-found treasure, they purchased a piece of land and then made a feast for their friends and distributed generously to their poor neighbours.

questions.question: How was the other couple that lived in the village where the old man and his wife lived? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.explanation: In the same village, there lived a wicked old man and his wife who were very greedy and insensitive to others' needs.

questions.question: What did the cruel couple do to Muko in hope that it would find a treasure for them?

options.option: They set other dainties before it
They coaxed the dog into their garden
They set bits of fish before it

questions.explanation: The wicked couple coaxed the dog into their garden and set bits of fish and other dainties before him to lure him to find the treasure for them.

questions.question: How did Muko feel when the wicked couple tried to lure him with good food?
questions.explanation: The dog was scared of the cruel couple, so, it neither moved nor ate the dainties offered to it by them.

questions.question: What did the cruel couple find beneath the surface of digging? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.explanation: The wicked old man began to dig but unfortunately found nothing but a dead kitten buried near the pine tree in the garden.

questions.question: What was the reaction of the cruel old couple on seeing the dead kitten in the plot, the ashes that made trees bloom?
questions.explanation: The wicked couple on seeing the dead kitten got enraged as they expected some treasure instead. The old man started thrashing the poor animal and his wicked wife chopped the dog's head out of rage.

questions.question: Who nearly chopped off the dog's head? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.explanation: The wicked old woman who happened to be a neighbour of the dog's masters nearly chopped off the poor animal's head as it failed to help her in finding treasure.

questions.question: Why did the neighbours kill the dog in the plot, The ashes that made trees bloom?

options.option: They wanted to eat the dog
The dog had lost its mind
The dog failed to find the treasure for them

questions.explanation: The greedy old couple coaxed the dog into their garden and lured him with dainties, so, that the dog would help them to find treasure buried inside the garden and become rich. The dog failed to do so and in a fit of rage, they killed the poor animal mercilessly.

questions.question: What did the pastry and sauce turn into as the greedy couple began to pound and grind them?
questions.explanation: The stingy and wicked couple borrowed the magic mill from their neighbour and began to pound and grind the rice and beans. But at the first blow and turn, the pastry and sauce turned into a foul mass of worms.

questions.question: When the pastry was ready:

options.option: the fireplace was clean and tidy

questions.explanation: The farmer and his wife baked the pastry and suddenly the whole mass (it) changed into gold coins. When the old woman took the hand-mill and filling it with beans began to grind, the gold dropped like rain.

questions.question: The mill was turned ________.
questions.explanation: The jealous old neighbour noticed the old couple getting rich again. So they also did the same. But their pastry and sauce turned into worms. They destroyed the mill borrowed from the old couple and burnt it. Thus, the mill was turned into ashes.

questions.question: Daimio was delighted to see the:
questions.explanation: Daimio was delighted to see the cherry tree blossom. The delighted daimio ordered the train to be stopped and got out to see the wonder. He thanked the man, offered him presents and also invited him to the castle.

questions.question: The dog's master:
questions.explanation: The good old man scattered the ashes over the withered cherry tree and suddenly it burst into blossom. The landlord got out to see the wonder. He thanked the man, offered him presents and also invited him to the castle.

questions.question: The greedy neighbour: (The ashes that made trees bloom)

questions.question: Who resides in the anthills? (The tiny teacher)

questions.question: What can we learn from the tiny teacher?

questions.question: How do ants greet the other ants? (The tiny teacher)

questions.question: How do ants feel on meeting other ants in the anthills?

questions.question: What was the old man's name in the plot, Chandni?

questions.question: What did Freedom mean, according to Abbu khan in the plot, Chandni?

questions.question: Why did Abbu Khan's goats run away?

options.option: For they were made to work very hard
For they wanted to be free
For they were not fed properly
For they were beaten by Abbu khan

questions.explanation: The goats ran away from Abbu Khan's compound and went to the hilly slopes to gain freedom. They all desired to dance in the green fields, admire nature's beauty and be happy without any restrictions.

questions.question: What was Chandni's colour?
questions.explanation: Chandni was very pretty and it was white as snow. Abbu Khan called her Chandni, which meant 'moonlight'.

questions.question: Who has written the story, Chandni?

questions.question: Where did Abbu khan live?
questions.explanation: Abbu Khan, an old man lived all alone in Almora, a town with a picturesque view of Himalayas. His only companions were his pet goats whom he treated as his own children.

questions.question: Which animal killed all of Abbu Khan's pet goats?
questions.explanation: All goats owned by Abbu khan were brutally attacked and killed by an old wolf that lived in the hills.

questions.question: How did Abbu khan feel when his goats ran away to the hills?
questions.explanation: Whenever Abbu Khan's goat disappeared and ran away to the hills, he felt very sad and lonely as the goats were his only companions and he loved them like his own children.

questions.question: What did Chandni watch every morning?

options.option: Abbu Khan offering his morning prayers

questions.question: What happened to Chandni when she saw the grassy hilltops?

options.option: She ceased listening to Abbu Khan's stories

questions.explanation: Chandni wanted to experience and enjoy freedom. She made up her mind to enjoy the bounties of nature. The grassy hilltops motivated her to break all the shackles and be free-spirited.

questions.question: What did Chandni urge to Abbu khan?

options.option: To allow her to go to the hills
To feed her with the juiciest grass
To take her for an excursion

questions.question: Who asked Chandni to join their group on the grassy hilltop?

questions.question: Where did Abbu khan decide to keep Chandni to save her from going to the hills?

questions.question: How did Chandni escape from the small hut?
questions.explanation: Chandni managed to escape from the small hut where she was captivated by Abbu khan. He had locked her in the hut but forgot to close a small window at the back. The same night Chandni made that window her passage to freedom.

questions.question: How did Chandni feel on reaching the grassy hilltop?
questions.explanation: Chandni was overwhelmed with joy on reaching the grassy fields. She felt as if the hills welcomed her. She felt like a child meeting her parents after years of separation.

questions.question: How long did the wolf and Chandni fight?

options.option: They fought for one whole day

questions.explanation: The wolf attacked Chandni, so, she fought with him full night like a warrior. The fight between the two went on through the night till a faint light appeared in the east. The first rays of the sun saw Chandni lying on the ground, completely soaked in blood.

questions.question: Abbu Khan's goats were ________.

questions.question: What was the name of the new goat?
questions.explanation: The new goat was very pretty. She was white as snow. Abbu Khan called her Chandni, which means &lsquo;moonlight&rsquo;.

questions.question: Who stopped eating the grass?
questions.explanation: Chandni stopped eating the green grass Abbu Khan brought for her.

questions.question: Who lived on the hills? (Chandni)
questions.explanation: Abbu Khan warned Chandni that there was a dangerous wolf in the hills who would eat her up if she went up to the hill.

questions.explanation: Abbu Khan forgot to close the small window at the back. The same night Chandni made that window her passage to freedom.
